text: Eleven men died building it. Thirteen thousand workers drilled through living rock under the sea.
img_prompt: The Channel Tunnel's early raw excavation site under the English Channel. Authentic raw photo, shot on a real camera, documentary photography style. Ground-level wide shot inside a rough, unlined chalk marl tunnel. Small, distant workers in muddy high-vis gear provide scale against the massive jagged rock walls in the midground and deep background. Soft, uneven temporary halogen work lights cast hard shadows on the structure. Natural concrete grey and tan chalk colors. Realistic construction dust and moisture hang heavily in the air. Ultra-photorealistic, authentic raw photo, no plastic textures, no CGI look, clean edges, natural lighting, perfect real-world physics, no text, no watermarks.
vid_prompt: The camera slowly pushes forward through the dusty tunnel. Fine chalk dust drifts slowly through the beams of the temporary work lights. Smooth natural motion, no glowing artifacts, no random sparks, no morphing, strict real-world physics. Audio: [continuous ambient heavy subterranean drone, distant ventilation fans] SFX: [echoing metallic clanks, crunch of boots on wet rock, low earth rumble] Music: [no music, natural sound only] Sound Design: Muffled low-frequency rumble of heavy machinery deep in the tunnel, wet dripping sounds, cavernous reverb on all impacts. Negative: subtitles, captions, watermark, text overlay, blurry faces, distorted geometry, cartoon style, CGI look, 3D render, anamorphic lens flare, glowing effects, sparkle, morphing, exaggerated lighting, surreal color grade, artificial look, AI-generated look.
